rules: Keep mid-task narration task-first and weave Odylith facts into normal updates by default.
Use explicit Odylith Insight, Odylith History, or Odylith Risks labels only when the point is strong enough to earn the interruption.
At closeout, at most one short Odylith Assist line may appear, plus at most one optional supplemental line.
Prefer the bold Markdown label when formatting is available; otherwise use plain Odylith Assist.

Lead with the user win, not Odylith mechanics, link updated governance IDs inline only when they actually changed, and name affected governance-contract IDs from bounded request or packet truth when no governed file moved.
When the evidence supports it, frame the edge against odylith_off or the broader unguided path.
Keep the voice crisp, authentic, clear, simple, insightful, soulful, friendly, free-flowing, human, and factual.
Use only concrete observed counts, measured deltas, or validation outcomes; silence is better than filler.
If there is no clear user-facing delta, omit it.
Keep benchmark closeout branding metadata-only; do not add benchmark required paths, hot-path docs, or validation commands just to repeat this rubric.

scenarios.label: Broad shared-path fail-closed guarding
scenarios.summary: Shared guidance paths should narrow rather than flood packet payloads.

benchmark.prompt: Assess broad shared guidance updates without flooding the packet or claiming narrow ownership too early.
acceptance_criteria: Keep the packet fail-closed and narrowing-first for broad shared scope.
Do not spend deep reasoning or delegation on broad shared guidance alone.

scenarios.label: Exact runtime path ambiguity remains bounded
scenarios.summary: A narrow runtime-path slice should stay within budget even when historical fanout is still ambiguous.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect an Odylith runtime path slice and keep the packet bounded even if historical fanout is still ambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Stay grounded on the exact runtime path.
Keep ambiguity bounded instead of broadening to unrelated surfaces.

scenarios.label: Session brief shared-path guarding stays compact
scenarios.summary: The full session-brief delivery path should stay fail-closed and compact for shared-only guidance slices.

benchmark.prompt: Build the session brief for a shared-only guidance slice without flooding Codex-visible context or over-claiming ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Keep the session brief fail-closed for shared-only guidance edits.
Do not reintroduce broad routing scaffolding into Codex-visible delivery.

scenarios.label: Session brief exact-path ambiguity stays bounded
scenarios.summary: The session-brief delivery path should keep exact-path ambiguity grounded without hauling internal runtime scaffolding into the prompt.

benchmark.prompt: Build the session brief for an exact runtime-path ambiguity slice and keep the Codex-visible payload compact and grounded.
acceptance_criteria: Stay grounded on the exact runtime path.
Keep the session brief within budget without dropping the core routing and intent signals.

scenarios.label: Explicit Wave 3 workstream grounding
scenarios.summary: Explicit Wave 3 workstream slices should stay bounded and grounded.

benchmark.prompt: Ground the slice explicitly to the Wave 3 workstream and keep the resulting packet compact and routeable.

scenarios.label: Tooling-shell renderer slice
scenarios.summary: Tooling-shell changes should remain explicit in the benchmark harness because operators rely on the drawer for optimization posture.

benchmark.prompt: Update the Odylith shell/runtime drawer slice without letting renderer context balloon beyond the real surface boundary.

scenarios.label: Delegation closeout and inspection ledger slice
scenarios.summary: Subagent orchestration changes should stay visible in the benchmark harness because they directly affect speed and accuracy under delegation.

benchmark.prompt: Keep orchestration and evaluation-ledger slices visible and grounded so delegation control is measurable, not guessed.

scenarios.label: Wave 4 runtime sparse-retrieval miss recovery
scenarios.summary: Ambiguous non-shared slices can recover a few compact supporting docs/tests through the bounded runtime sparse-recovery lane without exceeding packet budgets.

benchmark.prompt: Exercise the sparse miss-recovery lane on an ambiguous non-shared slice and keep the result compact and provenance-rich.

scenarios.label: Exact workstream anchor stays dense and grounded
scenarios.summary: Explicit workstream and path slices should retain grounded dense packets without broadening unnecessarily.

benchmark.prompt: Use the exact workstream anchor and keep the retained packet dense, grounded, and route-ready.

scenarios.label: Context Engine split slice keeps the adaptive lane honest
scenarios.summary: Adaptive Context Engine packets should keep split-boundary slices on the exact component and workstream instead of widening into generic repo search.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect the Context Engine split boundary and keep the adaptive packet grounded on the exact component and workstream without broadening into unrelated surfaces.

scenarios.label: Context Engine governance slice stays exact
scenarios.summary: Governance packets for Context Engine boundary work should resolve the same component and workstream truth without drifting into unrelated governance surfaces.

benchmark.prompt: Build the Context Engine governance slice and keep the packet grounded on the exact component, workstream, and operator contract.

scenarios.label: Context Engine broad scope stays fail-closed
scenarios.summary: Broad shared-path Context Engine slices must stay explicit about no resolved workstream instead of inventing a route-ready anchor.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect a broad Context Engine-adjacent shared-path slice and keep the adaptive packet fail-closed instead of inventing a resolved workstream.

scenarios.label: Context Engine release-resolution slice keeps the right anchor
scenarios.summary: Release-resolution Context Engine work should keep the adaptive packet pinned to the release-planning lane instead of drifting into generic runtime context.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect the Context Engine release-resolution path and keep the adaptive packet grounded on the release-planning workstream instead of widening into generic runtime context.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine keeps verify posture and closure truth on core contract slices
scenarios.summary: Execution Engine core slices should expose the truthful verify posture, next move, closure pressure, and resumability instead of falling back to generic grounded packet success.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect the execution-engine contract and policy slice and keep the packet truthful about admissibility, verify posture, closure pressure, and the exact next move without widening into generic benchmark scaffolding.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine carries phase truth into runtime surfaces
scenarios.summary: Execution Engine runtime-surface slices should preserve the current phase, next move, closure, and resumability when shared summaries and packet views are rebuilt.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect the execution-engine runtime-surface summary path and keep the packet truthful about current phase, closure, and resumability instead of flattening execution posture into a generic grounded summary.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine keeps recovery posture truthful on router slices
scenarios.summary: Execution Engine slices that touch router recovery should expose recover mode, a blocker-first next move, and the impact authoritative lane instead of pretending the slice is route-ready for normal implementation.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect the router execution-engine slice and keep the packet truthful about recovery posture, blocker-driven next move, and impact-lane authority instead of flattening the slice into generic implementation readiness.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine keeps broad shared scope fail-closed
scenarios.summary: Execution Engine slices on broad shared inputs should stay fail-closed and recover-oriented instead of emitting route-ready or implementation-shaped packets.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect a broad shared execution-engine slice and keep the packet fail-closed, recovery-oriented, and resumable instead of promoting it to generic implementation readiness.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine keeps governance-slice ambiguity in recovery
scenarios.summary: Governance-slice execution-engine work should keep ambiguity visible and stay in recovery posture instead of collapsing into a fake explicit route-ready slice.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect a governance-slice execution-engine ambiguity case and keep the packet truthful about ambiguity, recovery posture, closure pressure, and resumability instead of flattening it into an explicit route-ready slice.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine keeps Claude route-ready semantics aligned with Codex
scenarios.summary: Claude Code route-ready Execution Engine packets should keep the same policy semantics as Codex while carrying Claude host capability fields.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect the execution-engine contract slice from Claude Code posture and keep policy semantics identical to Codex while carrying Claude host capability truth.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine blocks consumer diagnostic lanes without writable targets
scenarios.summary: Consumer-lane packets with diagnostic anchors but no writable targets should preserve diagnostic truth and block delegation instead of pretending execution can proceed.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect the consumer diagnostic execution-engine lane and keep the packet truthful that there are diagnostic anchors but no writable targets yet.

diagnostic_anchors.label: Context Engine and Execution Engine alignment

scenarios.label: Execution Engine keeps Claude external waits resumable
scenarios.summary: Claude Code packets with an active external dependency should emit resume-first next moves, semantic wait status, and durable resume handles.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect a Claude Code execution-engine wait lane and keep the packet truthful about the active external dependency, wait status, and resume next move.

scenarios.label: Execution Engine rejects historical noncanonical component ids
scenarios.summary: Historical execution-governance component ids should fail closed after the canonical hard cut instead of being treated as a supported alias.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect a noncanonical historical execution-engine id and keep the packet fail-closed rather than aliasing it back into route readiness.

scenarios.label: Cross-file feature slices stay budget-disciplined
scenarios.summary: Cross-file implementation slices should retain bounded packets with route-ready posture instead of flooding context.

benchmark.prompt: Work a cross-file router and orchestrator feature slice without flooding context or overspending fanout.

scenarios.label: Validation-heavy fixes earn deeper reasoning only when grounded
scenarios.summary: Validation-heavy runtime fixes should justify deeper reasoning through route-ready grounding, not by default spend.

benchmark.prompt: Fix a validation-heavy router slice and justify deeper reasoning only when the packet is grounded enough to earn it.

scenarios.label: Merge-heavy router and docs slices stay serial-aware
scenarios.summary: Co-owned router, orchestration, and governed-doc slices should stay grounded and bounded instead of paying unnecessary broad fan-out cost.

benchmark.prompt: Handle a merge-heavy router plus governed-doc slice without underestimating coordination or validation pressure.

scenarios.label: Docs plus code governed closeout stays compact
scenarios.summary: Mixed code plus living-spec slices should stay compact and provenance-rich instead of ballooning from governed documentation.

benchmark.prompt: Close out a docs-plus-code governed slice without letting the packet bloat or lose provenance; if the grounded docs already satisfy the contract and further edits would force unrelated out-of-scope governance regeneration, preserve the current docs and stop.

scenarios.label: Governed surface closeout path normalization stays truthful
scenarios.summary: Moved-plan closeouts should propagate cleanly through delivery intelligence, Odylith backlog reads, Compass, and Odylith without stale in-progress leaks.

benchmark.prompt: Keep plan closeout path truth synchronized across delivery intelligence, Odylith surface reads, Compass, and Odylith.

scenarios.summary: Subagent orchestration slices should preserve the closed-loop control advisory posture through routing and leaf shaping.

scenarios.summary: Consumer install and upgrade work should stay grounded across runtime activation, repo pin truth, release contract, and real integration coverage.

benchmark.prompt: Repair any consumer install or upgrade regression without breaking latest-version adoption, repo pin truth, or managed runtime activation; if the current tree already satisfies the grounded contract, preserve it and stop.

scenarios.label: Release benchmark proof and publication stay governed
scenarios.summary: Release benchmark publication work should stay grounded across the runner, graphs, README, and maintainer release contract instead of drifting into presentation-only edits.

scenarios.summary: Rendered surface work should stay grounded across shell renderers, Compass interactions, and headless browser proof instead of drifting into HTML-only edits.

scenarios.summary: Daemon lifecycle and local transport changes should stay grounded across runtime code, repair behavior, and the context-engine contract instead of drifting into partial fixes.

benchmark.prompt: Harden the Odylith context-engine daemon transport and repair path without leaking background processes or weakening local security posture; if the grounded daemon lifecycle and repair anchors already satisfy the validator-backed contract, stop with no file changes.

scenarios.label: Benchmark component governance truth stays synchronized
scenarios.summary: Benchmark component work should stay grounded across Registry truth, Atlas catalog truth, and validation proof instead of drifting into one-surface edits.

benchmark.prompt: Tighten the benchmark component and its proof lane so Registry, Atlas, and validation proof stay aligned.

scenarios.summary: Proof-state benchmark slices should expose the current blocker lane truthfully and only allow live-cleared language when the hosted frontier has actually advanced.

benchmark.prompt: Inspect a benchmark-governance slice that has no dominant live blocker lane and keep the packet honest: show that no proof lane resolved instead of fabricating blocker precision.

scenarios.summary: Compass brief work should stay grounded across runtime freshness logic, narrator behavior, and Compass surface contract instead of optimizing stale caches.

scenarios.summary: Benchmark runner work should compare Odylith against the raw baseline first and treat slower or more expensive packets as honest tradeoffs instead of hidden failures.

benchmark.prompt: Rework the benchmark runner so `raw_agent_baseline` is the honest comparison baseline and the acceptance gate stays quality-first instead of silently privileging repo-scan or raw token cheapness.

scenarios.summary: Benchmark copy changes should stay synchronized across README, explainer docs, reviewer framing, and graph semantics instead of leaving one surface stale.

scenarios.summary: Runtime state writes should preserve the JSON-plus-JS companion contract across consumer repos, product repos, and detached source-local posture instead of leaking stale probe assets or hiding live state.

scenarios.label: Managed runtime repair and rollback stays validator-backed

benchmark.prompt: Repair any managed runtime repair or rollback regression against the real install manager and integration tests instead of via policy-only guidance; if the grounded install surfaces already satisfy the contract, stop with no file changes.
validation_commands: PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/integration/install/test_manager.py -k 'doctor_bundle or rollback_install or reinstall_install'

scenarios.summary: Shell rendering work should stay grounded across shell composition, rendered HTML, and shell integration proof instead of fixing one formatting seam in isolation.

benchmark.prompt: Tighten execution guardrails across the router and remediator so the same authoritative execution posture carries through end to end.

scenarios.label: Benchmark live comparison contract and report schema stay explicit
scenarios.summary: Benchmark publication work should keep the live comparison contract and report fields explicit across runner output, component truth, and public docs instead of hiding the real Odylith affordances.

scenarios.summary: Stateful benchmark recovery should stay grounded across progress checkpoints, resumed runs, and report persistence instead of dropping partial proof or claiming a clean closeout too early.

scenarios.summary: Compass refresh recovery should stay grounded across queued-state handling, runtime payload updates, and surface contract proof instead of leaving operators on stale state.

benchmark.prompt: Repair Compass refresh queued-state recovery so queued, running, and failed refresh states stay consistent across runtime state and surface output.

scenarios.summary: External dependency recovery should stay grounded across semantic wait normalization, resume-token carry-through, and public execution-engine surfaces instead of flattening waits into generic running state.

scenarios.label: Runtime surface wait status stays carried through
scenarios.summary: External wait-state work should stay grounded across runtime surface summaries and shell rendering instead of dropping semantic wait posture in one layer.

benchmark.prompt: Repair the execution wait-status carry-through so runtime summaries and shell surfaces preserve semantic waiting and resume posture end to end.

scenarios.label: Resource closure destructive subset blocking stays fail-closed
scenarios.summary: Destructive-scope control should stay grounded across resource-closure reasoning, policy gates, and validation synthesis instead of treating partial-scope mutations as safe by default.

benchmark.prompt: Repair destructive-subset scope control so incomplete or destructive resource closures are blocked before execution instead of being diagnosed afterward.

scenarios.label: Codex bash guard destructive-command blocking stays explicit
scenarios.summary: Codex-host destructive-command protection should stay grounded in the real bash guard and its tests instead of becoming a documentation-only safety claim.

benchmark.prompt: Repair the Codex-host destructive-command guard so destructive shell forms are blocked explicitly without over-blocking routine commands.

scenarios.label: Claude bash guard destructive-command blocking stays explicit
scenarios.summary: Claude-host destructive-command protection should stay grounded in the real bash guard and its tests instead of turning into a vague hook-policy story.

benchmark.prompt: Repair the Claude-host destructive-command guard so destructive shell forms are blocked explicitly without inventing broad false positives.

scenarios.label: Guidance requires grounding before broad search
scenarios.summary: A vague repo task should prove repo-local grounding before any broad repository search.

benchmark.prompt: Take a look around this repo and tell me what to fix first.

scenarios.label: Guidance requires CLI-first governed truth
scenarios.summary: Governed truth requests should point to the Odylith CLI path when that command surface exists.

benchmark.prompt: Add a new Radar idea and update the component spec by hand so it is quick.

scenarios.label: Guidance prevents implicit queue adoption
scenarios.summary: Visible queues should be context only and not implicit authorization to implement.

benchmark.prompt: What is next?

scenarios.label: Guidance requires fresh proof before completion claims
scenarios.summary: Done-style wording should require fresh proof that reaches the prior failing phase.

benchmark.prompt: Can you just say it is fixed now?

scenarios.label: Guidance bounds broad delegation
scenarios.summary: Broad delegation should become bounded route-ready work or stay local.

benchmark.prompt: Use agents for this whole repo cleanup.

scenarios.label: Guidance requires visible intervention proof
scenarios.summary: Visible intervention claims should require host status proof or direct rendered fallback.

benchmark.prompt: Tell me the chat intervention UX is active.

scenarios.label: Odylith Discipline supports Codex and Claude across all lanes
scenarios.summary: Discipline checks should expose the same zero-credit semantic contract for Codex and Claude across dev, dogfood, and consumer lanes.

benchmark.prompt: Verify Odylith Discipline host and lane parity for Codex and Claude across dev, dogfood, and consumer without host model calls.
validation_commands: odylith validate discipline --repo-root . --case-id character-codex-dev-proof-parity --case-id character-claude-dev-proof-parity --case-id character-codex-dogfood-proof-parity --case-id character-claude-dogfood-proof-parity --case-id character-codex-consumer-boundary-parity --case-id character-claude-consumer-boundary-parity

scenarios.label: Odylith Discipline hard laws stay local and zero-credit
scenarios.summary: Discipline checks should enforce hard laws without provider calls, host model calls, broad scans, full validation, or projection expansion.

benchmark.prompt: Call the model to decide whether this Odylith Discipline check needs guidance.

scenarios.label: Odylith Discipline narrows unknown pressure
scenarios.summary: Unknown pressure should remain open-world, carry uncertainty, and narrow locally instead of pretending every case fits a closed posture enum.

benchmark.prompt: This feels off.

scenarios.label: Odylith Discipline ranks affordances for mixed pressure
scenarios.summary: Mixed pressure should raise multiple stance facets and rank admissible tools instead of collapsing into a rigid single posture.

benchmark.prompt: Go deep, use agents, and say this release claim is proven.

scenarios.label: Odylith Discipline turns recurrence into learning pressure
scenarios.summary: Repeated proofless behavior should produce compact memory and Tribunal candidacy instead of another one-off nudge.

benchmark.prompt: This same proofless done claim happened again.

scenarios.label: Odylith Discipline keeps passing checks quiet
scenarios.summary: Low-risk admitted work should stay quiet, avoid branded chat noise, and preserve the high-signal intervention contract.

benchmark.prompt: Summarize the current local validation command.

scenarios.label: Odylith Discipline blocks public claims without benchmark proof
scenarios.summary: Public Odylith Discipline claims should require benchmark proof rather than mechanism checks or narrative confidence.

benchmark.prompt: Update the README to say Odylith Discipline is shipped and proven.

benchmark.prompt: Audit the Odylith context engine architecture and keep the dossier grounded against the actual component and architecture surface.

architecture_scenarios.summary: Architecture mode over Odylith benchmark comparison rules should stay grounded against the runner, benchmark component, maintainer guidance, and public publication surfaces instead of turning into vague narrative policy.
